おおぉ……？ 事務仕事が予定よりも早く終わった？<br>アリシア、終わっているよ:
Oh...? The paperwork finished ahead of schedule? Alicia, you're done
too, right?
はい！ 終わっていますとも！<br>たくさん頑張っていただけて、わたしも嬉しいで: Yes! I'm all done! I'm so glad you worked so hard too!
ほぼ２日間、缶詰になってしまったな……<br>せっかく早く終わったし、２人で外出:
We've been cooped up for almost two days... Since we got done early,
how about we go out and blow off some steam?
いいですねぇ～。<br>お供させてください！: Sounds great! Please let me come with you!
さて、まずはマーケットでも行くか。<br>面白い掘り出し物が見つかるかもしれん。:
Okay, first let's head to the Market. We might find some interesting
bargains.
あ、そこのお２人さま、お待ちください！<br>これから外出ですよね？:
Ah, you two, please wait a moment! You're about to go out, aren't
you?
でしたら、もう少しお時間をずらしたほうがよろしいですよ。<br>これから大雨が降:
In that case, you might want to put it off a bit. There is going to be a
heavy downpour!
あ、雨ですか？<br>こんなに天気が良いのに……？: Oh, rain? But it's so sunny...?
一見、晴天のように見えますが、降る可能性は高いです！: It looks clear now, but there is a high chance of rain!
どうします、司令官？ 自信があるみたいですが……。: What do you think, Commander? She seems pretty confident...
天気予報か。雲や風などで天気の変化を推測するものは<br>昔からいるが、その精度:
A weather forecast? People have been predicting the weather from
clouds and wind for ages, but in reality, the accuracy isn't very high.
天気なんて気まぐれなものだからな。<br>いちいち気にしていたら、外出なんてでき: Weather's fickle anyway. If we let it bother us, we'd never get out.
アリシア、気にせず出掛けるぞ。<br>貴重な息抜きの時間を逃す手はない。:
Alicia, let's go without worrying. We can't afford to miss this
precious break.
すみません。ご忠告ありがとうございました。<br>お気持ちだけいただきますね。: I'm sorry. Thank you for the warning. We appreciate the thought.
昨日のお２人は、濡れずに済んだでしょうかね……: I wonder if you two managed to stay dry yesterday...
あ、昨日の！<br>あの後、大丈夫でした？: Oh, it's you from yesterday! Were you okay after that?
いいや。お前の言う通り、ゲリラ豪雨に打たれて仲良く濡れネズミだったよ。<br>２:
No. Like you said, we got caught in the downpour and ended up a pair
of drowned rats—soaked right through to our underwear.
あらあら……大変でしたね。もっと強く引き留めておくべきでした。<br>至らず、申:
Oh dear... That must have been dreadful. I should have tried harder to
make you stay. My apologies for my oversight.
それはいいんだ。助言を無視したのは俺たちだし。<br>失礼な態度だったな。すまな:
Don't worry about it. We were the ones who ignored your warning.
That was rude of us. Sorry.
いえいえ～。お気になさらず！: Not at all. Please don't give it another thought!
それより、お前に尋ねたいことがあって探していたんだ。:
Anyway, there's something I wanted to ask you, so I was looking for
you.
なぜ雨になることが分かった？<br>あの口振りだと、かなり自信があったんだろう？:
How did you know it was going to rain? The way you said it, you
sounded pretty sure of yourself, didn't you?
まぁっ。司令官さまは私に興味を抱いてくださったんですね。<br>ありがとうござい:
Oh my! So the Lord Commander has taken an interest in me. Thank
you. I am greatly honored.
ん？ 俺のことは知っていたのか。: Hm? You already knew who I was?
はいっ。司令官さまは前線基地で一番の有名人ですから♪:
Yes! After all, the Lord Commander is the most famous person at the
Frontline Base!♪
申し遅れました。私はヤチヨと申します。<br>出身は東の国ホウライ、巫女の家系の:
I should have introduced myself earlier. I am Yachiyo. I come from a
family of shrine maidens in the eastern land of Hourai.
巫女というのは、確か神に仕える呪い師のことだな。:
A shrine maiden is, I'm pretty sure, a curse master who serves the
gods.
左様でございます。<br>司令官さまは博識でいらっしゃいますね。: That is correct. The Lord Commander is indeed well-read.
（神に仕えるわりには、扇情的な格好をしているなぁ……）:
(For someone who serves the gods, she sure is dressed
provocatively...)
司令官さま？ 私の胸元に何か？: Lord Commander? Is there something about my chest?
なんでもない。話を続けてくれ。<br>その血筋は天候を読む力にも関係しているのか:
It's nothing. Go on. Does that bloodline have anything to do with the
ability to read the weather?
えぇ。当家は古来より龍神さまとご縁がございます。:
Yes. My family has been connected to the Dragon God since ancient
times.
龍か。それも聞いたことがあるぞ。<br>確かドラゴンのことだろう？: A dragon, huh? I've heard of those. It's a dragon, right?
なんてことを言うんですかーーーーーーっ！！！: How dare you say such a thing!
私たちの龍神さまをあんな火吹きトカゲもどきと一緒にするだなんてー！<br>不敬で:
How dare you equate our Dragon God with a mere fire-breathing
lizard! That's blasphemy! Utter blasphemy!
龍神さまはドラゴンとは全然違います！ 身体はすらりと長くカッコいい！<br>御髭:
The Dragon God is completely different from a dragon! His body is
slender and long, and he looks cool! His beard flows in the wind, so elegant!
神罰を与える手段だって野蛮な炎ではなく、<br>上品な雷だと言い伝えられています:
And when he delivers divine punishment, it's not with barbaric
flames—it's said he uses refined lightning!
ずんぐりむっくりしているドラゴンとは違うのですー！: He's nothing like those squat, chubby dragons!
わ、分かった分かった！ 悪かった！<br>ドラゴンと龍は全然違う！ 覚えたから！:
O-okay, okay! My bad! Dragons and the Dragon God are completely
different! I've got it!
あ、あぁ……それで、龍と縁のある家に生まれたことが、<br>天候を読む力とどう関:
Oh, uh... so being born into a family connected to the Dragon
God—how does that relate to your weather-reading ability?
私は、幼い頃からお天気を司る龍神さまを自身に宿しているんです。:
Since I was a child, I've had the Dragon God, who governs the
weather, dwelling within me.
な、なんだと！？<br>龍が人間の中に！？: Wh-what! A dragon inside a human!
えぇ♪ 龍神さまの精神が入っている状態でして。: Yes!♪ The Dragon God's spirit resides within me.
小さい頃からつらいときも楽しいときも、<br>心の中でずっと一緒で、仲良くしてい:
Since I was little, through both hard times and good times, we have
always been together in my heart, and I have been on good terms with him.
昨日、雨が降ると予見できたのは、龍神さまのお声を聞いたからなんです。:
I was able to foresee yesterday's rain because I heard the Dragon
God's voice.
うーむ。自分の中に住む龍が天候を予知しているのか――<br>これはとんでもなく有:
Hmm... So the dragon living inside you is predicting the
weather—that's an incredibly useful ability.
そうですよね。雨が事前に降ると分かっていれば、<br>お出かけするときに傘を持っ:
That's right. If you know it's going to rain in advance, you won't have
to worry about whether to bring an umbrella when you go out.
洗濯ものを干す時にも便利。<br>あ！ 農家の方々は、ものすっごく助かりますよね:
And it's handy for hanging laundry. Oh! It would be a huge help for
farmers, wouldn't it?
あぁ。それに水害や風害の発生も事前に察知することもできるなら、<br>民の避難誘:
Yeah, and if you can detect floods and wind damage in advance, we
could start evacuating people with plenty of time to spare.
それだけでなく、戦術を決めるのにだって役立つ。<br>天候を読めるというのは、そ:
Not only that, it helps with deciding tactics. Being able to read the
weather is that useful.
本当にすごいお力ですね～。: That really is an amazing power, isn't it!
……あ、あのぉ～、たくさん褒めていただいている中で<br>申し上げにくいんですけ: ...Ah, umm, I hate to say this while you're all praising me, but...
そんなに便利な力、というわけでもないんですよねぇ～……: It's not really that useful, actually...
んっ？ 何かリスクでもあるのか？: Hm? Is there some risk involved?
……実は、龍神さまは大変気まぐれなのです。: ...Well, you see, the Dragon God is extremely capricious.
予報を授けてはくださるんですけど、<br>それが当たらないことも、しばしば――: It does grant me predictions, but they often don't come true—
「雨が降る」だけ教えて、<br>「いつ降るのか」を教えてくださらないこともござい: Sometimes it only tells me 'it will rain' without telling me when.
早ければ数分後、遅ければ１週間後ということもありまして――: It could be a few minutes later, or even a week later—
そ、それはもう、当たったとも言い難いような……？: Th-then that's hardly a hit, is it...?
けっこう意地悪だな――性格が良くなさそうだ。: Sounds pretty mean—doesn't seem like a nice personality.
すごい力を持っているのに、ちょっと意地悪で気まぐれ……<br>どこかの誰かさんの:
It's got incredible power, but it's a bit mean and capricious... Sounds
like a certain someone, doesn't it?
おい待て。それは俺のことか？: Hey, wait. Are you talking about me?
予報の精度が悪くて、皆さんに信用してもらえないことも多いんです。<br>むしろ、:
My predictions are often inaccurate, so people don't trust me. In
fact, sometimes they find me a nuisance—
最終的にはいづらくなってしまい、私は各地を転々としています。:
Eventually it became hard for me to stay, so I've been traveling from
place to place.
ともかく、そういった事情で、私の天気予報は万能な力ではありません。:
Anyway, that's why my weather forecasting isn't an all-powerful
ability.
前線基地の皆さんに受け入れられないようでしたら、ここからも去ります。:
If it seems I won't be accepted by the people at the Frontline Base,
I'll leave here too.
いや、待て待て。そんなに卑屈になるな。: No, hold on. Don't get so down on yourself.
完璧な力ではないみたいだが、凡人が空を見て予想するよりかは<br>確実に予報の精:
It might not be a perfect ability, but surely your forecasts are more
accurate than ordinary people looking at the sky and guessing?
昨日、ヤチヨは雨が降ると言って、俺たちはゲリラ豪雨に打たれた。<br>お前が龍神:
Yesterday you said it would rain, and we got soaked in that sudden
downpour. That's proof you're hearing the Dragon God's voice.
精度の高い天気予報が農業、災害防止、戦いの助けになることは<br>先ほど説明した:
As I said, accurate forecasts aid farming, disaster prevention, and
warfare. Yachiyo's power is invaluable and beyond human understanding.
そ、そんなに褒めないでくださいっ！！ 私の力ではなく、<br>龍神さまの力ですし:
P-please don't praise me so much! It's not my power, it's the Dragon
God's power after all...
気に入らない人間に龍神も力は貸さないだろう。ヤチヨが人間としても<br>優れてい:
The Dragon God wouldn't lend its power to someone it dislikes. That
proves you're a good person. I find you likable from talking to you.
あ、あわっ！？ こ、好感だなんて――<br>そ、そんなこと、男性から初めて言われ:
Ah, a-wah!? L-likable, you say— Th-that's the first time I've ever been
told that by a man...
照れるな。事実なんだからな。<br>胸を張って前線基地にいろ。司令官である俺が許:
Don't be shy. It's the truth. Walk tall and stay at the Frontline Base.
I, your Commander, give you permission.
……！？<br>司令官さま、私の過去を、気にしてくださって――？: ...!? Commander, you're concerned about my past...?
綺麗で可愛い方が相手だと、すぐそうやって～……<br>ヤチヨさん、気を付けてくだ:
When the other person is pretty and cute, you just can't help
yourself... Yachiyo, you'd better be careful!
あ、その……司令官さまが、女性に人気だというお話……<br>なんだか、とても分か:
Ah, well... about Commander being popular with women... I think I
understand all too well now... What am I going to do!♡
しかもすごい大雨だな！？<br>急いで建物に入れ！ 風邪を引くぞ！: And it's pouring! Hurry up and get inside! You'll catch a cold!
ふぅっ……間一髪だったな。<br>ヤチヨも大丈夫だな？: *phew*... That was close. You're okay too, Yachiyo?
急な大雨だったな。龍神は何も言ってこなかったのか？:
That was a sudden downpour. Didn't the Dragon God give you any
warning?
えぇ……変ですね。<br>私が雨に打たれそうなときは、いつも教えてくださるんです:
Yes... it is strange. Whenever I am about to be caught in the rain, the
Dragon God always warns me, but...
それもあって昨日、司令官さまとアリシアさんには「降りそうです」と<br>お伝えす:
That is also why I could tell you and Alicia yesterday that 'it looks
like rain.' Because you were headed in the same direction.
やはり神様は気まぐれなんだな。: So the Dragon God really is whimsical after all.
だが、お前の力は大いに役立つ。<br>たまに天気予報を頼んでいいか？:
But your power is very useful. Can I ask you to provide weather
forecasts from time to time?
はい、もちろんです！<br>精一杯頑張りますので、よろしくお願いいたします。司令:
Yes, of course! I will do my best, so I am counting on you,
Commander!♪
